text.title: Competitive Intelligence: Competitor Landscape Mapping
text.summary: Four competitor types, 2x2 positioning matrix methodology, whitespace identification rules, and orchestrator prompt pattern for the landscape-mapping sub-methodology.

rule.statement: Every competitor list must include all four types: direct (same solution, same customer), indirect (different solution, same problem), substitute (alternative approach entirely), and potential (could enter market). Reject any list missing substitute and indirect rows.
rule.rationale: Teams default to 3-5 obvious head-on competitors. Substitutes (spreadsheets, email, "do nothing") are the actual churn cause but rarely surface from a Google query — they must be explicitly prompted.

example.note: Project management tools: Direct (Asana, Monday, ClickUp), Indirect (Spreadsheets, Slack, Email), Substitute (pen and paper, meeting-based coordination), Potential (AI-native PM tools, no-code builders). Whitespace identified: AI-native PM for solopreneurs.

Project management tools: Direct only (Asana, Monday, ClickUp, Notion, Linear). Missing indirect, substitute, and potential types — misses the "do nothing" and "spreadsheet" competition that drives 60% of SMB status quo.

rule.statement: Pre-commit the two positioning axes with named exemplars for each pole before running the matrix. Do not let the agent choose axes that maximize visual separation after seeing the data.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc axis selection produces a map that looks clean but reflects LLM output aesthetics, not buyer decision criteria. Example of correct anchoring: x-axis "simple" = Notion-shaped vs "complex" = Salesforce-shaped.

rule.statement: After identifying whitespace, pair every candidate whitespace cell with a demand-validation step before acting on it. An empty cell is empty for a reason 80% of the time.
rule.rationale: Whitespace may be empty because: no demand, technically hard, regulated, or unprofitable. A cell being empty is a hypothesis to test, not a greenlight.

section.title: Orchestrator Prompt Pattern
section.p: Seed with category name. Produce 15-20 competitors covering all four types. For each, output a structured row with name, type, founded, funding_usd, team_size, pricing (model, entry_usd, url), positioning (≤140 chars), top_features, and evidence_urls (≥2, at least one third-party). Unknown fields → "unknown". No marketing copy verbatim.

rule.statement: Require evidence_urls length ≥2 with at least one third-party source per competitor row. Reject any row with only the competitor's own marketing site as evidence.
rule.rationale: LLMs paraphrase the competitor's homepage tagline as "positioning" and call the matrix done. Third-party evidence (review site, community thread, screenshot) provides independent verification.

text.reference: Competitor landscape table template with all four types
